cancer_history_summary:
Age: 19  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Osteosarcoma (high‑grade conventional) of the distal right femur  
Histology: Conventional high‑grade osteosarcoma  
Current extent: Oligometastatic – solitary pulmonary metastasis in the right lower lobe (2.0 × 1.8 cm) identified on 2025‑06‑25 CT (progression)  

Biomarkers: No genomic or protein biomarker results reported to date; comprehensive sarcoma‑focused next‑generation sequencing ordered but pending.  

Treatment history:  
# 2024 (date not specified): Limb‑sparing surgical resection of distal right femur osteosarcoma (curative intent).  
# 2024‑2025 (dates not specified): Adjuvant MAP chemotherapy (methotrexate, doxorubicin, cisplatin) – six cycles completed, concluded prior to June 2025, with no major complications.  
# Early 2025 (approximately May‑June 2025, exact dates not recorded): Ifosfamide + Etoposide – two cycles administered; subsequent surveillance CT on 2025‑06‑25 demonstrated radiographic progression (new solitary right lower‑lobe nodule).  

Planned/intermediate steps (not yet initiated): Video‑assisted thoracoscopic wedge resection versus stereotactic body radiation therapy (SBRT) under consideration; biopsy with NGS planned; counseling regarding fertility preservation discussed.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No documentation of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, renal dysfunction, hepatic dysfunction, HIV infection, or hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 68  
Sex: Male  

Cancer type: Cutaneous malignant melanoma (left upper extremity)  
Histology: Not explicitly stated (melanoma)  
Current extent: Metastatic (bilateral pulmonary metastases) – partial response to therapy as of latest imaging (largest nodule 11 mm, down from 16 mm)  

Biomarkers: BRAFV600E mutation (NRAS wild‑type)  

Treatment history:  
# Surgical resection – wide local excision with sentinel lymph node dissection (diagnosis at age 65, circa 2022). Pathology showed metastatic involvement of 2/3 nodes with extracapsular extension, staged IIIC.  
# Adjuvant pembrolizumab 200 mg IV q3 wk – initiated after surgery, completed 12 months of therapy (approximately 2024‑05 to 2025‑05). Achieved disease‑free status until PET‑CT on 2025‑06‑22 revealed new pulmonary nodules indicating progression. Toxicities: Grade 2 dermatitis at month 6, resolved with topical steroids.  
# First-line systemic relapse therapy – combined ipilimumab 3 mg/kg IV + nivolumab 1 mg/kg IV every 21 days, begun after 2025‑06‑22 assessment. Planned four induction cycles followed by nivolumab maintenance.  
 • Cycle 1: administered early July 2025, tolerated with Grade 1 pruritus.  
 • Cycle 2: administered early August 2025, uneventful; labs normal, LDH decreased to 185 U/L. Imaging 6 weeks after start (2025‑06‑23 CT) showed ≈33 % reduction in pulmonary nodules → Partial Response (RECIST v1.1).  
 • Cycle 3: administered early September 2025; ≈10 days later patient developed Grade 3 immune‑mediated colitis requiring hospital admission (reported on 2025‑06‑25 note). Colitis resolved with supportive care.  
# Modification after colitis: Discontinued ipilimumab permanently; continued nivolumab monotherapy 240 mg IV q2 weeks beginning after recovery (maintained beyond 2025‑06‑25). Ongoing response maintained; no new thoracic disease reported.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence:
No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases.  
No documentation of congestive heart failure.  
No pneumonitis noted on imaging or exam.  
Renal function normal (Creatinine 0.92 mg/dL).  
Liver enzymes within reference range (AST 31 U/L, ALT 34 U/L, bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL).  
No recorded HIV or hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 55  
Sex: Female  
Cancer type: Ovarian cancer  
Histology: Low‑grade serous carcinoma (LGSC)  
Current extent: Recurrent disease with measurable peritoneal implants and pelvic sidewall/para‑aortic lymph node involvement (no distant thoracic, bone or other organ metastases identified as of 06/2025)  
Biomarkers: KRAS p.G12D dominant clone (VAF ≈ 45 %); co‑existent NRAS Q61K subclone (low VAF); CCNE1 copy‑number gain (~3‑fold); classified as platinum‑refractory LGSC  

Treatment history:  
# 2023 (approximately, age 53) – Cytoreductive surgery: total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo‑ovariectomy, comprehensive surgical staging.  
# 2023‑04 to 2024‑04 – Adjuvant chemotherapy: carboplatin (AUC 5) + paclitaxel (175 mg/m²) IV, 4 cycles; completed ~April 2024; toxicity grade 2 nausea, otherwise well tolerated.  
# 2025‑03‑01 present: Endocrine therapy – letrozole 2.5 mg PO daily, initiated March 2025 (≈3 months prior to first follow‑up note); continued uninterrupted through June 2025; adverse effect grade 1 arthralgia managed with acetaminophen PRN; associated with stabilization of peritoneal disease on imaging and modest symptom improvement.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence:
No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases.  
Measurable disease is present (e.g., 1.7 × 1.4 cm FDG‑avid pelvic sidewall lesion, SUVmax 7.8 on 06‑22‑2025 PET/CT).  
No documentation of congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, significant renal dysfunction (creatinine 0.85 mg/dL on 06‑23‑2025 lab), hepatic dysfunction (AST/ALT within normal limits), HIV infection, or hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 45  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: T‑cell lymphoblastic lymphoma (mediastinal primary)  
Histology: TdT⁺, CD3⁺, CD7⁺ T‑cell lymphoblasts; CD20⁻, CD19⁻, CD22⁻  
Current extent: Metastatic disease with residual mediastinal mass, bilateral pulmonary nodules, multifocal hepatic lesions, probable splenic and bone‑marrow involvement (Stage IV biologic behavior)  
Biomarkers: NOTCH1 V1578F mutation; PTEN loss; JAK3 V678M mutation; TAL1‑ST5 fusion; CD3⁺, CD7⁺, TdT⁺, CD20⁻, CD19⁻, CD22⁻  

Treatment history:  
# Front‑line Hyper‑CVAD ± high‑dose MTX/cytarabine – 4 cycles (administered prior to 2025; achieved partial response, mediastinal mass decreased from ≈9 cm to 4 cm)  
# Salvage nelarabine – 2 cycles (administered after Hyper‑CVAD; resulted in stable disease)  
# Palliative mediastinal external beam radiation – 30 Gy in 10 fractions, completed circa May‑June 2025 (reduced mediastinal mass to ≈3 cm)  
# Anticoagulation for right popliteal/femoral DVT – therapeutic enoxaparin 1 mg/kg SC q12h started ≈2025‑05‑11, transitioned to apixaban 5 mg PO BID (ongoing)  
# Prednisone 40 mg PO daily ×5 days with taper (started 2025‑06‑22 for hepatic inflammation; discontinued after taper)  
# Dose‑adjusted bortezomib 1.0 mg/m² SQ Days 1, 8, 15 of 28‑day cycle (planned after 2025‑06‑22 visit; vincristine held after cycle 1 if neuropathy worsens) – initiation pending  

Planned/considered investigations/treatments (not yet commenced): gamma‑secretase inhibitor (NOTCH1‑mutant directed), ruxolitinib (JAK3‑mutant), pembrolizumab off‑label, various early‑phase T‑cell‑focused trials (CAR‑T, bispecifics, PI3Kδ inhibition).
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: Evidence of liver dysfunction – grade 3 transaminitis attributed to hepatic infiltration observed on laboratory testing concurrent with abdominal MRI (documented 2025‑06‑22) and reiterated in hepatoprocedural consult (2025‑06‑24). No documented uncontrolled CNS metastases, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, renal insufficiency, or HIV/hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 76  
Sex: Female  
Cancer type: Ovarian cancer  
Histology: High‑grade serous carcinoma  
Current extent: FIGO Stage IV metastatic disease, hepatic metastases and formerly involved para‑aortic nodal disease now radiographically stable/post‑treated  
Biomarkers: CA‑125 48 U/mL (stable vs prior 44 U/mL)  

Treatment history:  
# 2024‑present: Rucaparib 600 mg PO twice daily (maintenance PARP inhibition) – ongoing, tolerating grade 2 anemia/fatigue, no new toxicities reported  
# ≈ March 2025: Percutaneous radiofrequency ablation of dominant hepatic metastasis (initial size 4.2 cm → post‑ablation 3.8 cm with necrosis) – single procedure, resulted in expected inflammatory changes, disease otherwise stable  
# ≈ April 2025: Stereotactic body radiation therapy (SBRT) to solitary para‑aortic lymph node, 30 Gy delivered in 5 fractions – completed, subsequent imaging shows fibrosis without residual activity
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, significant renal dysfunction (creatinine 0.84 mg/dL on 05/2025), clinically meaningful liver dysfunction (AST 38 U/L, ALT 42 U/L, ALKP 112 U/L, bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL on 05/2025), HIV infection, or hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 70  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Prostate cancer  
Histology: Adenocarcinoma, Gleason 9 (4+5)  
Current extent: Metastatic castration‑resistant prostate cancer, bone‑only disease, no visceral metastases (stable osseous lesions on bone scintigraphy and CT as of 25 Jun 2025)  

Biomarkers:   
- Serum PSA: 0.4 ng/mL (≈3 months prior) → 2.6 ng/mL (19 Jun 2025), >50 % rise indicating biochemical progression  
- Testosterone: castrate level <20 ng/dL (most recent assay)  
- No HER2, EGFR, ALK, ROS1, BRAF, MSI‑H, or PD‑L1 reported  
- Genomic profiling pending (DDR genes BRCA1/2, ATM, PALB2)  

Treatment history:  
# Date unknown – ? : Radical prostatectomy with extended pelvic lymph node dissection (curative intent)  
# Continuous (dates unclear, ongoing): Leuprolide depot intramuscular injections for medical castration (AD​T)  
# ≈06/2024 – 19 Jun 2025: Abiraterone acetate 1000 mg PO daily + Prednisone 5 mg BID (initiated for rising PSA and osseous disease) – tolerated with intermittent hot flashes; developed grade 2 essential hypertension managed with amlodipine 5 mg daily; demonstrated biochemical progression (PSA ↑) leading to discontinuation  
# 19 Jun 2025 present: Enzalutamide 160 mg PO daily (started after stopping abiraterone) – ongoing  
# Recommended (but not yet started): Docetaxel 75 mg/m² IV q21 days ± Prednisone 5 mg BID (for symptomatic bone disease or rapid PSA kinetics)  
# Considered (pending decision): Radium‑223 dichloride α‑emitter therapy for symptomatic bone‑dominant disease without visceral mets  
# Pending: Comprehensive next‑generation sequencing of archived primary tumor or ctDNA to evaluate eligibility for PARP inhibition
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence:
- No evidence of untreated or uncontrolled brain metastases (imaging reports make no reference to intracranial disease).  
- Disease is measurable biochemically (rising PSA) and radiographically (bone lesions), thus “lack of measurable disease” does not apply.  
- Cardiovascular: Controlled hypertension on amlodipine; heart size normal on CT, no reported congestive heart failure.  
- Pulmonary: No interstitial infiltrates, pneumonia, or drug‑induced pneumonitis on CT; bone scan report mentions only mild degenerative changes.  
- Hepatic: No hepatic toxicity observed during abiraterone therapy; liver enzymes not discussed, implying no clinically significant dysfunction.  
- Renal: No documentation of renal impairment.  
- Infectious: No history of HIV, hepatitis B/C, or other chronic viral infections recorded.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 63  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Small‑cell lung carcinoma (extensive‑stage)  
Histology: Small‑cell (neuroendocrine) carcinoma  
Current extent: Progressive disease – new solitary hepatic metastasis (segment VII, 1.6 cm, SUVmax 7.8) while prior thoracic primary, mediastinal nodes, left adrenal and right femoral lesions remain stable; performance status ECOG 1–2  

Biomarkers: None reported in the excerpt  

Treatment history:  
# Carboplatin AUC 5 + Etomidate (etoposide) 100 mg/m² Day 1‑3 every 21 days, total 4 cycles; concurrent palliative external beam radiation to L3 (single 8 Gy fraction). Achieved partial response ≈30 % volume reduction by Cycle 2 CT; subsequently stable disease. Toxicities: Grade 3 neutropenia (filgrastim support), Grade 3 thrombocytopenia requiring transfusion, Grade 2 peripheral neuropathy. Completed prior to June 2025.  
# Lurbinectedin 3.2 mg/m² intravenously every 21 days – initiated after platinum/etoposide course. Administered at least 5 cycles up to early 2025. Resulted in modest decrease of adrenal lesion size and stable femoral lesion; however developed Grade 2 anemia (treated with oral iron) and recurrent Grade 3 neutropenia prompting dose reduction after fifth cycle. Best outcome: stable disease until imaging showed new hepatic metastasis meeting RECIST criteria for progressive disease.  

(Amrubicin exposure referenced as part of prior therapy sequence, but specific dates, doses, and outcomes were not documented.)
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence:
- No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases (brain MRI repeatedly negative for intracranial disease).  
- No documented congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, renal insufficiency, or active HIV/Hepatitis infection.  
- Mild hepatic dysfunction attributed to metastatic liver involvement rather than pre‑existing liver disease.  
Overall, there is no indication of common boilerplate exclusion criteria.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 62  
Sex: Female  
Cancer type: Glioblastoma, IDH‑wildtype (right parietal lobe)  
Histology: WHO Grade IV astrocytic tumor (glioblastoma)  
Current extent: Recurrent/progressive disease with new non‑enhancing infiltrative focus (~2 cm) in right posterior parietal lobe; no distant extracranial metastases identified  

Biomarkers: IDH‑wildtype (reported)  

Treatment history:  
# Initial maximal safe resection of right parietal lesion (date not specified) – achieved residual postoperative changes, stable left temporal resection cavity thereafter.  
# Adjuvant radiotherapy (presumed standard external beam, dates not specified) – completed prior to subsequent therapies.  
# Temozolomide concurrent with radiotherapy followed by adjuvant cycles (dates not specified).  
# Experimental PDGFRα inhibitor combined with temozolomide (prior to 2025‑06‑20; exact start/stop dates unavailable) – deemed ineffective in halting disease spread.  
# Bevacizumab 10 mg/kg intravenously every 14 days initiated at age 63 (approx. early 2025) – produced transient symptomatic benefit; subsequently held owing to grade 3 proteinuria (dose interruption date not recorded).  
# 2025‑06‑25 – Resumption of bevacizumab monotherapy 10 mg/kg q14 days (ongoing as of last note).
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: Evidence of renal dysfunction – grade 3 proteinuria developed during bevacizumab therapy, necessitating temporary drug interruption (recorded in progress note dated 2025‑06‑25). No documentation of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, significant hepatic impairment, HIV, or hepatitis infections.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 66  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Breast cancer (right side)  
Histology: Invasive lobular carcinoma  
Current extent: Metastatic (liver and bone predominant) – disease classified as stable/partial response on current therapy  
Biomarkers: Hormone‑receptor positive, HER2‑low, PIK3CA E545K mutation, ESR1 D538G mutation; no HER2 amplification detected  

Treatment history:  
# ~2024 (patient aged 64) – Start of capecitabine 1250 mg/m² orally twice daily Days 1‑14 every 21 days; ongoing as of 6/20/2025. general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, clinically significant renal or hepatic dysfunction, HIV infection, or chronic viral hepatitis.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 34  
Sex: Male  

Cancer type: Appendiceal well‑differentiated gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumour (NET)  
Histology: Well‑differentiated, Grade 2 (Ki‑67 ≈ 8 %)  

Current extent: Metastatic to liver (two known hepatic lesions, segments III and VI); disease stable on current therapy, no extra‑hepatic disease detected on recent imaging.  

Biomarkers: Somatostatin receptor‑2 strongly positive (⁶⁸Ga‑DOTATATE PET SUVmax ≈ 12); Ki‑67 ≈ 8 %; no other molecular alterations reported.  

Treatment history:  
# Age 24 (circa 2019): Surgical resection of appendix (stage IA, pT1 N0 M0).  
# Ages 25‑27 (approximately 2020‑2022): Temozolomide + Bevacizumab – achieved partial radiographic response.  
# After completion of chemotherapy (around 2022): Initiated octreotide LAR 30 mg intramuscular every 28 days following confirmation of SSTR2 positivity. Continued long‑term.  
# Approximate age 31 (early 2024): Received Lu‑177‑DOTATATE peptide receptor radionuclide therapy (PRRT). Resulted in regression of a hepatic lesion (segment III) to ≤0.5 cm and durable control of peritoneal deposits.  
# March 2025 (patient aged 33): Started everolimus 10 mg orally daily combined with continued octreotide LAR 30 mg q28 days (off‑label combination).  
# 03/2025 present: Everolimus + Octreotide – ongoing; most recent MRI (05/2025) shows both hepatic lesions stable in size, meeting RECIST criteria for stable disease.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, significant renal dysfunction, severe liver dysfunction, HIV infection, or hepatitis infection documented in the supplied records.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 64  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Gastro‑esophageal junction adenocarcinoma  
Histology: Adenocarcinoma  
Current extent: Metastatic disease with peritoneal carcinomatosis, malignant ascites, multiple bilobar hepatic metastases (largest 4.1 cm, stable), mildly enlarged para‑aortic and iliac lymph nodes, previously irradiated L3 bony lesion (osteoblastic, no new bone disease)  

Biomarkers: HER2 positive (IHC 3+, ISH amplification); co‑amplifications MET and FGFR2; KRAS p.G12D mutation; PIK3CA p.H1047R mutation; PD‑L1 combined positivity score (CPS) 15  

Treatment history:  
# First‑line (approximately age 58): capecitabine + oxaliplatin (CAPOX) plus trastuzumab – achieved disease stabilization.  
# Second‑line (following CAPOX progression, dates unspecified): FOLFIRI regimen – progressed systemically.  
# Third‑line (initiated ≤early 2025, ongoing through 6/25/2025): pembrolizumab (Keytruda) 200 mg IV every 3 weeks – initially stable, now radiographic progression with increasing peritoneal disease and ascites; last infusion administered 6 days prior to 6/25/2025.  
# Local therapy: stereotactic external beam radiation to solitary L3 osteoblastic metastasis (date not specified) – demonstrated sclerosis, no further activity.  
Supportive care: levothyroxine 75 µg daily for grade 2 hypothyroidism; intermittent ondansetron for nausea; ferrous sulfate 325 mg BID begun after transfusion; red blood cell transfusions for grade 3 anemia; planning initiation of epoetin alfa 40,000 U SC weekly pending iron studies.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, significant renal dysfunction, clinically significant liver dysfunction, HIV infection, or hepatitis infection documented in the supplied records.
